text: We've been chasing the same dashboard for years. The pipeline-coverage view always lies because the cohort definition keeps drifting on us.
This. Plus our forecasting model is six steps removed from how reps actually qualify. Garbage in.
We tried Outreach for two years. Switched to a leaner setup and our seat costs dropped 40 percent without losing reach.
How is the new tool with multi-channel sequences? That's where Outreach was strongest for us.
The hook in this post landed because it named the rep's friction not the manager's dashboard. Most B2B copy gets this exactly backwards.
Same energy as our outbound thesis. Frontline rep voice beats VP voice on every social surface I've tested.
The teardown angle in tweet 3 of this thread is the move. They didn't pitch the product they pitched the change in buyer behavior.
Curious how this maps to category-leader posture vs challenger posture. Same play different result?
We're a 14-person team and we just need a sales motion that doesn't break in week 2. Saas-labs's vertical playbook is the closest thing we found.
That vertical playbook saved us 30 hours of consulting fees. Worth a deeper read.
Salesloft's rhythm cadence is fine but the AI summarization is half a step behind. The newer entrants are eating that lunch.
We replaced both Outreach and Gong this quarter and the consolidation argument held up. Two contracts smaller. Same pipeline coverage.
The 1.5 second hook on this clip nailed the friction. 'Your CRM thinks every deal is the same' is the line.
Felt this. Three quarters of our reporting is rebuilt every monday because the categories drift.
Just another saas tool with a fancy dashboard. Pass.
Gong's capture is still the best. Coaching layer is where most tools fall apart.
Honestly Gong's UI is starting to feel old. The new generation of tools have better mobile and faster summaries.
Best part of this thread is the data point in tweet 4. Not many B2B teams share their actual conversion numbers like this.
Genuine question: how do you maintain this cadence without burning out the writing capacity? That's the part most teams hide.
Apollo is good for prospecting. Bad for orchestration. The split shows up around month 4.
We pulled Apollo six months in. Replaced the orchestration layer with an internal tool. Saved $40K and we're not looking back.
Pipeline coverage that doesn't lie. That's the real product. Everything else is a wrapper.
This thread on the rep's friction landed because it didn't try to sell. It just named what the cohort already knows.
Our customers say the same thing. The reporting category drift is the silent killer.
Buy my course about saas marketing!
